prompt: A seamless repeating textile pattern designed as a four-way continuous wallpaper or fabric print, featuring vintage botanical rose bouquets in metallic antique gold on a deep midnight navy background. The composition should tile cleanly in all directions with no visible seams, arranged as an elegant all-over repeat of rose clusters, half-open blossoms, small buds, curling stems, and scattered leaves. Use a refined etched or jacquard-like linework texture so the flowers look slightly woven, embroidered, or engraved rather than painted. The bouquets should vary in scale from medium to large, with dense clusters alternating with smaller isolated blooms to create a balanced repeat. Keep the palette restricted to rich dark navy and luminous muted gold, with strong contrast and a luxurious, moody, classic baroque fashion-textile feel. Add very subtle vertical shadowed folds across the surface so it resembles draped fabric or a curtain, while preserving the seamless repeat structure. High detail, ornamental, elegant, regal, seamless pattern swatch, suitable for upholstery, wallpaper, or luxury dress fabric.
